why.title: Why {gold}GATE{/gold} Jerusalem?
why.locationDesc: At the entrance to Jerusalem, next to the String Bridge and government quarter. Walking distance to the Knesset, courts, Machane Yehuda Market and the Old City.
why.transportDesc: Yitzhak Navon Station (32 min to TLV), Green Line light rail opening 2026, 280 bus routes, cycling paths. Maximum accessibility.
why.business: Not Just a Tower — An Entire District
why.businessDesc: Part of a 1.2 million sqm mixed-use development featuring hotels, residential, a European-inspired commercial boulevard, cultural venues, and 20 towers. A complete business ecosystem with 60,000 jobs and 350,000 daily visitors.

location.title: Your Neighbors
location.subtitle: GATE Tower sits at the heart of Jerusalem's action — everything is a short walk or ride away.
location.trainHook: Yitzhak Navon Station is adjacent to the complex. Tel Aviv is a commute, not a trip.
location.lightRailHook: Green Line opening 2026. 3 lines, direct access from the tower.
location.knessetHook: The entire government quarter — your clients are right next door.
location.sacherParkHook: Jerusalem's green lung. Lunch break, morning run, or an outdoor meeting.
location.machaneYehudaHook: Business meetings with character. Restaurants, cafes, and Jerusalem atmosphere.
location.oldCityHook: 3,000 years of history, a short ride from the office.
location.greenLineTitle: The Green Line Light Rail Opens This Year
location.greenLineDesc: A new line connecting Malha to the city center via GATE Jerusalem — a station right next to the tower. Direct access to all of Jerusalem.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What does the floor price include?
faq.a1: The price includes the gross floor area (including common areas). Parking is priced separately — ₪250,000 + VAT per spot, 8 spots per floor.
faq.a2: Delivery is expected in 2029-2030, subject to construction progress.
faq.q3: Can I purchase part of a floor?
faq.a3: Each floor can be modularly divided into 8 office units (Models A-H). Individual units are available starting from 103 sqm net.
faq.a4: 30% upon signing the purchase agreement, 70% at delivery. The project is financed by Bank Hapoalim with bank guarantees for every buyer.
faq.a5: Underground parking with 8 spots per floor. Cost: ₪250,000 + VAT per parking spot.
faq.a6: Yes. The tower is designed to meet international LEED green building standards — energy efficiency, excellent air quality, and environmental responsibility.

quarter.title: Jerusalem Gateway {gold}Quarter{/gold}
quarter.subtitle: Israel's largest urban development project — a world-class financial, business & cultural center
quarter.description: The Jerusalem Gateway Quarter is an unprecedented national initiative promoted by the Jerusalem Municipality and the Israeli Government. Located between the Yitzhak Navon train station, the Knesset (Parliament), and the Supreme Court, the quarter is set to transform the city's entrance into a premier business, commercial, and cultural hub.
quarter.whyTitle: Why is the city promoting the Quarter?
quarter.whyDesc: Jerusalem is the capital, yet until now it has lacked a modern financial center. The Quarter aims to bridge that gap — attracting tech companies, international law and accounting firms, and global corporations, while creating 60,000 new jobs and significant municipal revenue.
quarter.plannedDesc: A European-inspired commercial boulevard, international hotels, cultural institutions including the new Bezalel Academy, government offices, courts, green parks, and residential buildings — a complete urban ecosystem operating 24/7.
